title: List account membership endpoints
description:
## What
Implement GET, POST, and DELETE /api/v1/lists/:id/accounts for adding, removing, and listing accounts in a list.

## Why
Lists are only useful once accounts can be placed in them; membership management is the core user interaction.

## Agent team pattern
- Spawn a Research subagent to: curl mastodon.social GET /api/v1/lists/:id/accounts to confirm it returns an array of Account objects with Link header pagination; curl POST /api/v1/lists/:id/accounts with body {account_ids:[]} to confirm request shape and 200 response on success; curl DELETE /api/v1/lists/:id/accounts to confirm it also takes body {account_ids:[]} and returns 200 with empty object; verify behavior when adding an unfollowed account (expect 422).
- Spawn an Implementation subagent to: implement GET /api/v1/lists/:id/accounts returning paginated Account objects from list_accounts JOIN accounts, with Link header using max_id/since_id on list_accounts.account_id; implement POST /api/v1/lists/:id/accounts which validates ownership, checks each account_id is followed by the authed persona (query follows table), inserts all valid into list_accounts in a single transaction, returns 422 with error message if any account is not followed; implement DELETE /api/v1/lists/:id/accounts which removes given account_ids from list_accounts in a single transaction, returns 200 {}.
- Spawn a Test subagent to: test GET returns Account objects matching mastodon.social Account shape; test POST with a followed account succeeds; test POST with unfollowed account returns 422; test DELETE removes the account; test pagination via Link header; test that operating on another persona's list returns 404.

## Test discipline
- Independent oracle: mastodon.social GET /api/v1/lists/:id/accounts live fixture for Account object shape; mastodon.social behavior for adding unfollowed account (observe 422 response).
- Test approach: create list, follow two accounts, POST both to list, GET list/accounts and assert both appear; DELETE one, GET again and assert only one remains; POST an unfollowed account_id, assert 422.

## Defensive programming checklist
- No `.unwrap()` in handler paths
- Can only add accounts you follow — enforce via follows table query before INSERT
- Adding an unfollowed account returns 422, not 500 or silent ignore
- All account_ids in a bulk add/remove are processed in a single transaction — partial success is not acceptable
- List ownership must be verified before any mutation
- account_id values from request body must be parsed as snowflake strings, not raw integers
